title: Stream Chat Aggregator Tutorial
subtitle: How creators combine Twitch, Kick, and X livestream chats into one OBS overlay
bullets: Website: https://web-iota-eight-87.vercel.app
Goal: one shared session, multiple creators, one overlay
Use this as a Loom-style submission walkthrough
narration: Hi, this is a quick walkthrough of the Stream Chat Aggregator. It lets streamers combine Twitch, Kick, and X live chats into one shared session and display everything in a single OBS or Streamlabs browser overlay.

title: 1. Register or Login
subtitle: Every creator gets their own account
bullets: Open the Vercel website
Create an account or log in
Each user owns their own platform connections
No one has to share passwords or tokens
narration: First, open the website and register or log in. Each creator has their own account. That means your Twitch, Kick, and X connections belong to your account only, and collaborators connect their own accounts separately.

title: 2. Create a Shared Chat Session
subtitle: The session is the shared room for all chats
bullets: Create a session name
Set your creator label, like Penpal
Select the session in the dashboard
The session becomes the center of the combined chat
narration: Next, create a Shared Chat Session. Think of this as the shared room. Give it a name and set your creator label. The overlay listens to this session, not just to one platform account.

title: 3. Connect Twitch, Kick, and X
subtitle: Each platform is connected by the creator who owns it
bullets: Twitch: open OAuth and authorize
Kick: enter the Kick channel name
X: paste the X broadcast link
The dashboard auto-refreshes and locks in the connection
narration: Now connect the platforms. Twitch uses the OAuth authorization flow. Kick uses the channel name. X uses the public broadcast URL. After connecting, the dashboard refreshes and locks in the saved connection so you can start listeners without refreshing manually.

title: 4. Invite Another Creator
subtitle: Collaborators join the same shared session
bullets: Use Invite collaborators
Enter their email and creator label
Send them the private invite link
They register or log in and click Accept invite
narration: To bring another creator into the same session, use the Invite collaborators section. Enter their email and a display label, then send them the private invite link. They register or log in, accept the invite, and the same session appears in their dashboard.

title: 5. Start Chat Listeners
subtitle: Multiple creators can run the same platform in one session
bullets: Start Twitch, Kick, or X for the selected session
Listeners are tracked by session, platform, and user
Penpal Twitch and Guest Twitch can both run
Errors now show clear messages instead of Bad Request
narration: After connections are saved, start the provider listeners. The system tracks listeners by session, platform, and user. So if two creators both have Twitch, both Twitch listeners can run in the same shared session without replacing each other.

title: 6. Create the OBS Overlay
subtitle: One browser source shows all chats
bullets: Click Create overlay URL
Copy the private Vercel overlay link
Add it to OBS or Streamlabs as a Browser Source
The overlay receives all active chats in the shared session
narration: Finally, create the OBS or Streamlabs overlay URL. Copy the private Vercel overlay link and add it as a browser source. That one overlay receives messages from all active collaborators and all active platforms in the shared session.

title: End-to-End Example
subtitle: What the final overlay combines
bullets: Penpal Twitch + Penpal Kick + Penpal X
Guest Twitch + Guest Kick + Guest X
All normalized into one feed
Ready to submit as the working tutorial demo
narration: In the final result, Penpal can have Twitch, Kick, and X connected, while a guest creator also has Twitch, Kick, and X connected. Every message is normalized into one feed and shown in one overlay. That is the full collaboration workflow.
